calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Converting Trust Into Unitrust Or Acknowledgment Of Resignation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-31-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: See minutes for this date in case PTR-15-298524, the matters were heard together.

Petition For Order Converting Trust Into Unitrust Or Acknowledgment Of Resignation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-24-2015 Continued To Nov-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR 15-298524 The Peter A. Salz Trust and PTR-15-298619 The Marital Trust.
Appearances: Thomas Huster for (petitioner) Elaine Salz. Rob Epstein for (remainder beneficiaries) Philip Salz, Roger Salz & Robert Salz. Evan Abrams for (trustee) First Republic.

Mr. Epstein reported that they conducted mediation on Friday, the issues are not resolved, that they are working to find another trustee, that Wells Fargo is willing but not until the litigation is complete and that they are waiting on written discovery. Mr. Huster & Mr. Abrams are in agreement. The parties agreed to an evidentiary hearing, expecting it to last 1-2 hours with no witnesses. The Petition to Determine Questions of construction of the trust instrument, etc., (case 298524) and the Petition for order converting trust into unitrust or acknowledgment of resignation of trustee (case 298619) were continued to November 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Converting Trust Into Unitrust Or Acknowledgment Of Resignation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-22-2015 Continued To Aug-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-15-298524 The Peter A. Salz Trust & PTR-15-298619 The Marital Trust created under the Peter A. Salz Trust.

Appearances: Thomas Huster for (petitioner) Elaine Salz. Robert Epstein for (remainder beneficiaries/objectors) Philip, Robert & Roger Salz. Evan Abrams for (trustee) First Republic.

The court addressed the pending petition and stated that if the trustee resigns there would be no authorization to make distributions to Ms. Salz. Mr. Abrams stated that they would like a receiver assigned. The court stated that it would need a petition on file for that relief, suggested mediation and told the parties that they need to do something regarding establishing a successor trustee. Mr. Epstein stated that he was hoping to be referred out to trial. Mr. Huster stated that the lifestyle of the parties is what is at issue and that they need a trustee that is amenable to the needs of Ms. Salz. Mr. Epstein stated that Philip Salz is a co-trustee in name, that he is empowered to select a successor trustee, that it must be a bank, that he is not optimistic of finding a replacement while in litigation, that at this moment it is not provident to replace or accept the resignation, that he is agreeable to mediation, that he would like to conduct discovery, that there is a debate as to how to apply the lifestyle clause at this time and that they need to obtain information from those who could shed light as to what the original intention of the trustor was. The Court stated that it was not inclined to set another hearing or trial, that it will only continue the matter with the understanding that the parties will mediate and resolve the issue of a successor trustee and the interpretation of the trust in regards to Ms. Salz. Mr. Abrams stated that the trustee wants out, that there is no code stating that they cannot be allowed to resign, that it is very difficult to administer this estate, that at one time the parties were close to finding a successor and that counsel objects to every decision the bank makes. Mr. Epstein stated that they should be done with the case in 60-90 days and does not see why the bank cannot stay on for that amount of time. The parties asked the court for a copy of the pro-bono mediators (understanding that this would not be pro-bono mediation) and were provided the list. The Petition to determine questions of construction of the trust instrument and to instruct the trustee to distribute appropriate amounts to E. Salz (case 298524) and the petition for order converting trust into unitrust or acknowledgment of resignation of trustee (case 298619) were continued to August 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Converting Trust Into Unitrust Or Acknowledgment Of Resignation Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-08-2015 Continued To Jun-22-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
